questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: The 'Sevilla Forum on Debt', recently seen in the news, is primarily associated with which of the following?

options.text: A World Bank initiative to restructure the sovereign debt of Low-Income Countries (LICs).
An outcome of the Fourth International Conference on Financing for Development (FfD4) to tackle debt crises in developing countries.
A G20 framework designed to provide a debt standstill for countries affected by climate disasters.
An IMF-led platform for private creditors to negotiate debt-for-nature swaps.

questions.explanation: The Sevilla Forum on Debt was launched at the 16th session of the UNCTAD to tackle the debt crisis in developing countries. It is led by Spain and is one of the outcomes of the Fourth International Conference on Financing for Development (FfD4) as part of the Sevilla Platform for Action.
questions.takeaway: The Sevilla Forum is a UN-supported (UNCTAD/UN DESA) platform led by Spain to address debt sustainability in developing nations.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The forum is launched at the 16th session of the UN Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD16) to tackle the entrenched debt crisis in developing countries... It is one of the outcomes of the Fourth International Conference on Financing for Development (FfD4) and part of Sevilla Platform for Action.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: The 'Druzhba Pipeline', often mentioned in the context of global energy security, primarily transports which commodity?
questions.explanation: The Druzhba (Friendship) pipeline is one of the largest crude oil pipeline networks in the world, carrying oil from Russia to various points in Europe including Belarus, Hungary, and Slovakia.
questions.takeaway: The Druzhba pipeline is a critical piece of infrastructure for Russian crude oil exports to Europe.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: About Druzhba (Friendship) Pipeline: It is one of the biggest crude oil pipeline networks in the world. The Druzhba pipeline begins in Russia and transports oil through Belarus...

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: The 'Sevilla Commitment', recently seen in the news, is primarily associated with which of the following?

options.text: A framework to close the annual SDG financing gap in developing countries.
A treaty to regulate the transboundary movement of hazardous electronic waste.
A maritime security agreement for the Mediterranean and Atlantic corridors.
A protocol for the management and disposition of weapons-grade plutonium.

questions.explanation: The Sevilla Commitment lays out a path to close the $4 trillion annual Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) financing gap in developing countries. It is the first inter-governmentally agreed financing for development framework since 2015.
questions.takeaway: The Sevilla Commitment is a landmark inter-governmental agreement focused on bridging the massive financing gap required for SDGs in the developing world.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Sevilla Commitment lays out a path to close the $4 trillion annual SDG financing gap in developing countries. It is the first inter-governmentally agreed financing for development framework since 2015.
